meta.version_quirks: Emerald merges Ruby and Sapphire content, adds the Battle Frontier, lets you catch both Groudon and Kyogre, introduces a fully animated overworld for Pokemon sprites, and features expanded contests and a refined story focused on Rayquaza.

starters.name: Treecko
strengths: Fastest of the three starters at every stage
Strong against Roxanne, Wattson (with Bullet Seed), Juan/Wallace, and Drake's Kingdra
Learns Leaf Blade naturally as Grovyle (Lv 29) which is one of the best moves in Gen 3
Pure Grass typing means only one weakness multiplier per type
weaknesses: Bad matchup against Flannery, Winona, Phoebe, Glacia, and most of the Elite Four
Movepool outside of Grass is shallow on the cartridge (mostly Crunch, Dragon Claw via TM)
Frail defensive stats; gets one-shot by stray Ice or Flying moves

early_game: Bullet Seed (TM09 from Cycling Road shop)

starters.best_for_new_players_rating: 6/10 - rewarding but punishing. Sceptile demands smart switching.
starters.fun_trivia: Sceptile was the first pure Grass-type starter final with a base 120 Speed stat, and its design was inspired by the Carolina anole lizard. In Gen 6 it gained a Mega Evolution that turned it Grass/Dragon.

starters.name: Torchic
starters.signature_evolution: Torchic -> Combusken (Lv 16, gains Fighting type) -> Blaziken (Lv 36)
strengths: Becomes Fire/Fighting at Combusken, which is incredible for a 4th badge run
Double Kick at Lv 16 trivializes Roxanne's gym
Sky Uppercut as Combusken hits Phoebe, Sidney, and Glacia
Blaze Kick + Sky Uppercut covers most of Hoenn's threats
weaknesses: Slow until Blaziken; outsped by many gym aces
Awful matchup against Wallace/Juan and Tate & Liza's Lunatone
4x weak to nothing but stacks Water/Ground/Flying/Psychic weaknesses
starters.best_for_new_players_rating: 9/10 - the easiest run in Emerald thanks to dual STAB and great coverage.
starters.fun_trivia: Blaziken was the first Fire/Fighting starter, kicking off a Gen-long tradition (Infernape, Emboar, Cinderace). It became the first Pokemon ever given a Mega Evolution in X/Y.

starters.name: Mudkip
starters.signature_evolution: Mudkip -> Marshtomp (Lv 16, gains Ground type) -> Swampert (Lv 36)
strengths: Becomes Water/Ground at Marshtomp - immune to Electric, only one weakness (4x Grass)
Solo's Roxanne, Wattson, Flannery, and Tate & Liza without breaking a sweat
Highest bulk of any starter; great for new players
Learns Earthquake, Surf, and Ice Beam by TM for full coverage
weaknesses: 4x weak to Grass moves - watch for Winona's no Grass, but Drake's Salamence and wild Ludicolo can sting
Predictable; many trainers carry Razor Leaf or Giga Drain
starters.fun_trivia: Swampert is one of only three Water/Ground Pokemon families in Gen 3 (with Whiscash and Quagsire). The 'So I herd u liek mudkipz' meme originated from a 4chan thread in 2007 and helped cement Mudkip in pop culture.

gym_leaders.name: Roxanne
gym_leaders.strategy: Nosepass has Harden and Rock Tomb. The Rock Tomb speed drop is what kills slow physical attackers, so prefer Special Attackers. A Magnemite caught at Cave of Origin route is too high level here, but a Lotad from Route 102 evolved to Lombre is excellent. Avoid using Torchic alone unless it has Double Kick (Lv 16, so train one extra level first).
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Boosts Attack stat in battle and lets you use HM01 Cut outside of battle.
gym_leaders.tip: Backtrack to Petalburg Woods after the gym to grab a Shroomish - it evolves into Breloom with Mach Punch by Lv 23.

gym_leaders.name: Brawly

team.key_move: Bulk Up + Arm Thrust + Vital Throw

gym_leaders.strategy: Makuhita uses Bulk Up to snowball. Don't let it set up - hit it on turn 1 with a strong Flying or Psychic attack. Taillow's Wing Attack and Quick Attack make this gym trivial. Beware Vital Throw, which never misses.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Pokemon up to Lv 30 obey, and you can use HM05 Flash outside battle.
gym_leaders.tip: Catch a Magnemite in New Mauville later - useless for Brawly, but you can grab a Whismur in Rusturf Tunnel that evolves into Loudred and learns Stomp.

gym_leaders.name: Wattson
gym_leaders.strategy: A Ground-type fully neutralizes Wattson. Marshtomp is the cleanest sweep in the game. Watch out for Voltorb's Self-Destruct - swap to a Rock or Steel type for the Boom turn. Magneton is Steel/Electric, so its only weakness here is Ground/Fire/Fighting.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Boosts Defense in battle and enables HM06 Rock Smash outside battle.
gym_leaders.tm_given: TM34 Shock Wave (a never-miss Electric attack)
gym_leaders.tip: After defeating Wattson, talk to him again - he gives you a Thunder Stone-related sidequest to clear out New Mauville's generators, rewarding TM24 Thunderbolt.

gym_leaders.name: Flannery
gym_leaders.strategy: Torkoal opens with Overheat which lowers its own Special Attack two stages, so you can stall through it. Watch for Attract, which neuters male attackers - bring a female Pokemon or Yellow Flute to break it. Numel and Camerupt are Fire/Ground, so Water moves do quadruple damage. Slugma is pure Fire.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Lets you use HM04 Strength outside battle and boosts Speed in battle.
gym_leaders.tip: Hot Springs in Lavaridge heal your team for free - park here while training. The old woman in town gives you an Egg containing Wynaut.

gym_leaders.name: Norman

team.key_move: Facade + Slack Off + Yawn (every other turn due to Truant)

gym_leaders.strategy: Slaking has Truant - it loafs every other turn. Use the loafing turn to set up Bulk Up, heal, or switch in a wall. Hariyama with Vital Throw and Belly Drum is the textbook counter. Don't put a Ghost type in - he carries Faint Attack. Slaking's Yawn forces switches; bring an Awakening or use a Pokemon with Insomnia/Vital Spirit.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Lets all Pokemon up to Lv 70 obey and enables HM03 Surf outside battle.
gym_leaders.tip: Norman is your father in this game - the dialogue around the gym fight is some of the best in the franchise. Mom calls you on the PokeNav after.

gym_leaders.name: Winona
gym_leaders.strategy: Altaria is Dragon/Flying, so Ice Beam is a one-shot. Skarmory is Steel/Flying - hit it with Fire or Electric, NOT Rock (it resists). Pelipper is doubly weak to Electric. The whole gym is Electric food - Manectric or Magneton solo it. Watch for Altaria's Dragon Dance.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Pokemon up to Lv 80 obey, and you can use HM02 Fly outside battle.
gym_leaders.tip: After Winona, head to Mt. Pyre next - this is where the team plot really kicks off and you'll need Fly to keep up with the legendary chase.

gym_leaders.name: Tate & Liza
gym_leaders.battle_format: DOUBLE BATTLE - this is the only mandatory double-battle gym in the series
gym_leaders.strategy: Lead with two Dark or Ghost types. Sharpedo's Crunch is a one-shot on either ace. Earthquake hits both opponents - perfect partners are Flygon, Camerupt, or Swampert paired with a Flying ally to dodge it. Beware: Solrock has Sunny Day + SolarBeam combo, and Lunatone has Hypnosis. Bring Awakenings.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Pokemon up to Lv 90 obey, and you can use HM08 Dive outside battle.
gym_leaders.tip: Pick up the Sun Stone from the meteorite-loving NPCs near the Space Center after the gym - it's the only one in the early-mid game.

gym_leaders.name: Wallace

team.warning: Whiscash is Water/Ground - Grass moves do 4x

gym_leaders.strategy: Note: in vanilla Emerald, Wallace is the gym leader and Juan is replaced. (In Ruby/Sapphire it was Wallace.) Milotic has Recover + Marvel Scale, so it's spongy. Status it with Toxic or burn, then chunk with Electric or Grass. Whiscash is doubly weak to Grass. Don't bring Mudkip line solo - Milotic outclasses it.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Pokemon of all levels obey, and you can use HM07 Waterfall outside battle.
gym_leaders.tip: After Wallace's gym, you trigger the Sootopolis Kyogre/Groudon climax. Stock up on Ultra Balls, Max Repels, and Revives at the Pokemart before entering Cave of Origin.

elite_four.name: Sidney
elite_four.key_strategy: Hariyama with Brick Break or Cross Chop, Blaziken with Sky Uppercut, or Heracross with Megahorn rolls this whole team. Watch for Swagger - it raises your Attack but confuses you. Bring a Persim Berry or use a Special Attacker. Shiftry's Fake Out forces a flinch turn 1, so don't lead with your sweeper at full HP commit.

elite_four.name: Phoebe
elite_four.key_strategy: Dark moves are super effective AND Ghost types take neutral damage from Normal (they're immune to Normal but Crunch/Bite are Dark, which deals super effective). Sharpedo's Crunch, Houndoom's Crunch, or Absol's Bite/Crunch all dismantle her. Sableye is the trickiest - it has no weakness on its dual typing (Dark/Ghost). Use Earthquake or wear it down with Toxic. Avoid Psychic-types entirely - Phoebe's Ghosts have Crunch/Faint Attack.

elite_four.name: Glacia
elite_four.key_strategy: All her Pokemon are pure Ice except Walrein (Ice/Water). Blaziken with Blaze Kick or Flamethrower obliterates the gym. Hariyama with Brick Break is also fantastic. Watch out for Walrein's Sheer Cold - it's a one-hit KO move. Walrein is doubly weak to Electric due to Water typing. Manectric or Magneton can revenge.

elite_four.name: Drake
elite_four.key_strategy: Ice Beam is your best friend. Walrein, Glalie, or Lapras (traded) work great. Salamence and Altaria are doubly weak to Ice (Dragon/Flying). Beware Earthquake on Flygon and Salamence - don't lead with Magneton. Kingdra is Dragon/Water and only weak to Dragon, so save it for last with a Dragon Claw user. Glacia's stolen Walrein from your prior fight should still have full Ice Beam PP if you slept through her with Soft-Boiled.

elite_four.name: Wallace
elite_four.title: Champion
elite_four.weakness: Mostly Electric/Grass, but Ludicolo (Water/Grass) is only weak to Bug/Flying/Poison
elite_four.key_strategy: Manectric or Raikou-tier Electric attackers shred most of his team. The catch is Ludicolo - bring Sceptile, Beautifly, or a Crobat to handle it. Whiscash is Water/Ground - 4x weak to Grass. Gyarados is 4x weak to Electric. Milotic with Recover + Marvel Scale is the wall - status it (paralyze with Thunder Wave) before chunking with Electric. Wallace is the only Champion in the franchise to specialize in Water.

legendaries.name: Groudon
legendaries.location: Terra Cave (roaming/anchor cave that appears in a random route after the Sootopolis storyline)
access_requirements: Beat the Elite Four
Speak to Professor Cozmo and check the news on TV in any Pokemon Center
Terra Cave's location rotates - check the Hoenn news report each day
legendaries.catching_tips: False Swipe with a Gallade/Sceptile to leave 1 HP, then status it (Hypnosis from Gardevoir or Thunder Wave). Groudon has Drought ability which boosts Fire moves and weakens Water moves, so don't try to chip with Surf. Use Ultra Balls or a Timer Ball after 10+ turns. Has Earthquake, Fire Blast, Hyper Beam, Bulk Up - strong and bulky.
legendaries.recommended_ball: Timer Ball (after turn 10) or Ultra Ball; Dusk Ball if you can wake it up at night

legendaries.name: Kyogre
legendaries.location: Marine Cave (roaming/anchor cave on a random water route after Sootopolis)
access_requirements: Beat the Elite Four
Marine Cave appears on a random water route; check Hoenn TV/news
Surf required to reach it
legendaries.catching_tips: Has Drizzle which forces rain, doubling its own Water move damage and weakening Fire. Don't bring a Fire attacker. Use a Whismur/Loudred with Sing for sleep or a Gengar with Hypnosis. Its moveset includes Sheer Cold (OHKO!), so save before engaging. Same Timer Ball strategy as Groudon.
legendaries.recommended_ball: Timer Ball or Net Ball (it's part Water)

legendaries.name: Rayquaza
legendaries.location: Sky Pillar (Route 131, accessible after the Sootopolis crisis)
access_requirements: Reach Sky Pillar via Mach Bike navigation - the cracked floors require speed
Climb 5 floors of bike puzzles
Story-mandatory encounter to settle Groudon vs Kyogre, but if you knock it out you can re-encounter it after Hall of Fame
legendaries.catching_tips: Highest base stat total of any Pokemon you can catch in the cartridge (680). Carries Outrage, Extreme Speed, Fly, Rest. Don't let Outrage lock it in if you're using a Dragon - the recoil-confusion is brutal. False Swipe doesn't work (Outrage hits hard back). Lower with strong neutral moves, then status (paralyze ideally - it can't sleep with Rest unless you remove Chesto Berry). Save before engaging.
legendaries.recommended_ball: Timer Ball is mathematically best after ~30 turns; Ultra Ball as backup

legendaries.name: Latios
legendaries.location: Roams Hoenn after the Hoenn TV news event with Mom (post-7th badge)
access_requirements: Watch the news at home with your mom after getting the 7th badge

Choose 'Blue' in the news for Latios (default in Emerald is Latios; Latias if you choose differently in the male/female protagonist version - actually in Emerald it's locked to Latios for male protagonist and Latias for female, with the other obtainable via the Eon Ticket event)
Eon Ticket (Mystery Gift event item) lets you sail to Southern Island for the OTHER Latios/Latias
legendaries.catching_tips: Roaming legendary - it flees on turn 1. Use Mean Look with Crobat (Lv 30+ learns it via breeding/move tutor) or Block. Repels block lower-level encounters so you only run into Latios when biking. Use Master Ball if you want to skip the chase entirely - this is the most justified Master Ball in the game.
legendaries.recommended_ball: Master Ball (highly recommended), or Net Ball after Mean Look

legendaries.name: Latias
legendaries.location: Same as Latios - which one roams depends on your news choice and Eon Ticket events
legendaries.catching_tips: Identical mechanics to Latios. Slightly more defensively oriented. Both have access to a Latiasite/Latiosite in later games but in Emerald you'll just want them for Soul Dew (held item that boosts their Sp. Atk and Sp. Def).
legendaries.recommended_ball: Master Ball or Net Ball after Mean Look

legendaries.name: Regirock
legendaries.location: Desert Ruins (Route 111, behind Mirage Tower area)
access_requirements: Catch a Relicanth and a Wailord, place them as the head/tail of your party
Read the Braille in Sealed Chamber (under Route 134 dive spot) - it tells you to 'go straight then right'
In the Desert Ruins chamber, walk 2 steps down from the center, then 2 right, then use Rock Smash
legendaries.catching_tips: Has Curse + Superpower + Explosion. Don't let it Explode - it WILL kill itself. Bring Pokemon with Damp ability (Poliwhirl/Wooper) to block Explosion. Use Sleep status; chip with Special Attackers since its Defense is 200 base.

legendaries.name: Regice
access_requirements: Same Relicanth/Wailord party requirement
Inside the cave, stand in the center for 2 minutes WITHOUT MOVING
(Some sources say walk in a counter-clockwise lap; the proven method is just stand still for 2 minutes)
legendaries.catching_tips: Massive Special Defense (200 base). Use physical attackers to chip it down - Hariyama with Brick Break, or a high-attack Steel-type. Has Explosion - use Damp Pokemon. Avoid Special moves; you'll do pitiful damage even with super effective.

legendaries.name: Registeel
access_requirements: Same Relicanth/Wailord party requirement
Use Fly to access Route 120, walk to the small tomb
Inside, use Flash in the center of the room
legendaries.catching_tips: Balanced 200/200 defenses. Status it (paralyze, then put to sleep) and chip with Fighting or Fire moves. Watch for Curse (slows it but raises attack/defense) and Explosion. Steel typing resists everything except Fire, Fighting, and Ground.

legendaries.name: Mew (event only)
legendaries.location: Faraway Island (event-only, requires the Old Sea Map item from a Nintendo distribution event in 2006)
legendaries.access_requirements: Old Sea Map distribution item; not legitimately obtainable today without trade or hacking
legendaries.catching_tips: Mew runs through tall grass on Faraway Island and you have to track it without it spotting you (Solid Snake style). Once cornered, it teleports. After enough loops, you battle it at Lv 30. Use False Swipe and a Quick Ball.
legendaries.recommended_ball: Quick Ball (turn 1) - in Gen 3 use Net Ball or Ultra Ball as Quick Ball doesn't exist

legendaries.name: Deoxys (event only)
legendaries.access_requirements: Aurora Ticket distribution event; involves a triangle puzzle on the island
legendaries.catching_tips: In Emerald, Deoxys appears in its Speed Forme by default (different from FireRed/LeafGreen). Has Extreme Speed. Save before the puzzle - solving it spawns Deoxys and you only get one shot. Use False Swipe and Ultra Balls.

hidden_secrets.title: Mirage Island
hidden_secrets.description: A tiny island that only appears off Route 130 if your party includes a Pokemon whose Personality Value matches the day's hidden randomly-generated PV (16 bits, so roughly 1-in-65,536 chance per day). The old man on Pacifidlog Town's leftmost house tells you whether it's there today. The only thing on the island is a wild Wynaut horde and a Liechi Berry. It's the rarest non-event location in the game.
hidden_secrets.tip: Build a team with as many Pokemon as possible cycled through to maximize chances. There's no way to game it - just check daily.

hidden_secrets.title: Trick House
hidden_secrets.description: Route 110, owned by the Trick Master. Each gym you beat unlocks a new puzzle floor. Rewards include TMs (TM12 Taunt, TM31 Brick Break, TM32 Double Team), the Hard Stone, and rare items. The first puzzle (a scroll-burning maze) rewards the Hard Stone.
hidden_secrets.tip: Re-enter after each badge - the Trick Master rebuilds the house with a new puzzle. The 8th puzzle gives a Magnet.

hidden_secrets.title: Berry Master's Wife (Route 123)
hidden_secrets.description: The Berry Master's wife gives you a rare berry each day if you tell her a phrase. Saying specific phrases gives specific rare berries:
hidden_secrets.tip: These rare berries can be planted and grown into infinite supplies - they're used to make max-power Pokeblocks for Contests.

hidden_secrets.title: Mossdeep Space Center NPCs
hidden_secrets.description: Talk to all the NPCs on the second floor of the Space Center. Once Team Magma's plot wraps, talk to the man in the upper-left desk - he gives you the Sun Stone if you've talked to enough scientists. The space center is also the only place in Emerald with NPC dialogue referencing Jirachi's myth.
hidden_secrets.tip: After the main story, the launch goes successfully and you can watch a rocket take off.

hidden_secrets.title: Lilycove Department Store Rooftop Lottery
hidden_secrets.description: On the rooftop of Lilycove Dept Store, there's a free vending sale during certain in-game events. Also: the Pokemon Lottery in the Pokemon Center checks all Pokemon in your PC for matching ID numbers. Trade with friends to multiply your odds - matching all 5 digits wins a Master Ball!
hidden_secrets.tip: Match 5 digits = Master Ball, 4 = PP Up, 3 = Exp Share, 2 = PP Up, 1 = nothing. Trade often.

hidden_secrets.title: Battle Frontier Symbols (post-game)
hidden_secrets.description: The Battle Frontier on the artificial island past Lilycove has 7 facilities, each with a Frontier Brain who gives a Symbol when defeated. Earning all 14 Symbols (Silver + Gold for each facility) is the true post-game completion. Frontier Brains: Anabel (Tower), Greta (Arena), Tucker (Dome), Lucy (Pike), Spenser (Palace), Brandon (Pyramid), Noland (Factory).
hidden_secrets.tip: Save before each Brain fight - you only get one shot per streak. Held items don't carry between Pyramid floors.

hidden_secrets.title: Sealed Chamber and Regi Trio
hidden_secrets.description: Under Route 134's dive spot, there's a chamber with Braille on the walls. You need a Wailord (Pokeball head of party) and a Relicanth (tail) to read the script. This unlocks the three Regi caves around Hoenn. The Braille on the walls is real Braille and translates to the actual instructions in English.
hidden_secrets.tip: Catch Relicanth via Dive on Route 124/126 - it's rare. Wailmer evolves into Wailord at Lv 40.

hidden_secrets.title: Secret Bases
hidden_secrets.description: Use Secret Power (TM43, given by an NPC in Slateport's market) on certain trees, bushes, walls, and dirt mounds to create a Secret Base. You can decorate it with dolls, mats, posters, and tents. Mix Records with friends via Link Cable to visit each others' bases - their teams become NPC trainers you can battle once per day for free EXP.
hidden_secrets.tip: Secret Bases are the original Pokemon Camp/Joint Avenue and one of the best sources of unlimited daily EXP if you have friends with Emerald carts.

hidden_secrets.title: Trainer Hill
hidden_secrets.description: Located between Mauville and Verdanturf via the cable car, Trainer Hill is a 4-floor sequential trainer rush. You earn record times. Custom trainer hills can be downloaded via the e-Reader (Japan/US event-only). Beating the default 3-trainer rooms gives a small reward, but the real prize is the speedrun leaderboard.
hidden_secrets.tip: Use Steel/Ground/Ghost coverage to handle the random trainer mix. Stock up on Ethers.

hidden_secrets.title: Move Tutors of Hoenn
hidden_secrets.description: Several NPCs across Hoenn teach moves for free or for cost:
tutors: Slateport Battle Tent area - Counter, Mimic
Fallarbor Town - teaches Draco Meteor-equivalent moves like Mega Punch/Mega Kick
Battle Frontier - Body Slam, Double-Edge, Seismic Toss, Dream Eater, Mega Punch/Kick, etc. (cost: Battle Points)
Lilycove - the Move Reminder for Heart Scales (find Heart Scales by smashing rocks or using Item Finder on the beach)
hidden_secrets.tip: Heart Scales are key for re-learning forgotten moves. Use Pickup-ability Pokemon (Linoone, Zigzagoon) to farm them.

hidden_secrets.title: Mauville Game Corner
hidden_secrets.description: Slot machines in Mauville City. Win Coins, exchange them for TMs (TM13 Ice Beam, TM23 Iron Tail, TM24 Thunderbolt, TM30 Shadow Ball, TM35 Flamethrower) or rare Pokemon (Abra, Dratini in trade games). Slots are RNG-based but the cherry/replay strategy improves odds.
hidden_secrets.tip: TM24 Thunderbolt is one of the best moves in Gen 3 and 4000 Coins. The 1000 Coin TM35 Flamethrower is also incredible.

hidden_secrets.title: Contest Halls
hidden_secrets.description: Lilycove (Master), Verdanturf (Normal), Slateport (Super), Fallarbor (Hyper). Five contest categories: Cool, Beauty, Cute, Smart, Tough. Win all 4 ranks in a category to unlock a Pokemon Picture in your Trainer Card and earn rare ribbons. Pokeblocks (made from berries with friends or PokeNav match calls) raise contest stats.
hidden_secrets.tip: Feebas evolves into Milotic only when its Beauty stat is maxed out via blue Pokeblocks, then leveling up. This is the only way to get Milotic in vanilla Emerald.

hidden_secrets.title: Feebas Tiles
hidden_secrets.description: Feebas only spawns on 6 specific tiles in Route 119, randomly chosen each game. You'll fish (Old/Good/Super Rod) on every tile until you find one with Feebas. Once found, save the location - they're consistent until you alter your Trainer ID.
hidden_secrets.tip: Use a Super Rod and bring a lot of patience. Plant Feebas tiles can be inherited from friends' Trainer IDs in some cases.

hidden_secrets.title: Trick Master TMs
hidden_secrets.description: Inside the Trick House, the Trick Master rewards puzzle completion with TMs you can't easily get elsewhere: TM12 Taunt, TM32 Double Team, TM31 Brick Break. The Brick Break is essential for Battle Frontier Screen-spammers.
hidden_secrets.tip: Use Cut and Strength frequently inside the puzzles - many tricks involve hidden items behind cuttable trees.

hidden_secrets.title: Pacifidlog Tides
hidden_secrets.description: Pacifidlog Town's currents shift directionally every few hours. The currents push you in specific directions which can either help (toward Sky Pillar) or hinder you. Use the Mach Bike to outrun them.
hidden_secrets.tip: If you're trying to reach Sky Pillar, the morning current is the most useful direction.

breeding_basics.where: Day Care on Route 117 (Mauville to Verdanturf path). Drop two compatible Pokemon (opposite genders, same Egg Group) and walk in steps. The old man outside paces - if he's facing the road, no egg yet. If he's facing the fence, egg is ready.
breeding_basics.ditto_role: Ditto can breed with any non-legendary, non-Ditto Pokemon. Trade one in from FireRed/LeafGreen Trainer Tower or Pokemon Mansion.
breeding_basics.egg_groups: Pokemon must share at least one Egg Group. Some Pokemon are in groups like 'Monster + Dragon' giving wide compatibility.
breeding_basics.everstone: If the FEMALE holds an Everstone, the offspring inherits her Nature 50% of the time. This is THE primary way to get a specific Nature in Gen 3.
inheritance: The species comes from the female (or non-Ditto if Ditto is involved)
TMs/Egg Moves: father teaches compatible TMs and special egg moves to the offspring
IVs: 3 IVs are randomly inherited from parents in Gen 3 (more in later gens). Random across HP/Atk/Def/SpA/SpD/Spe.

iv_basics.what_are_ivs: Individual Values - hidden 0-31 stats per Pokemon for each stat (HP, Atk, Def, SpA, SpD, Spe). They're randomly assigned at capture/hatch and never change. Higher IV = higher final stat.
iv_basics.checking_ivs: No in-game IV judge in Emerald. Use online IV calculators with your Pokemon's level/nature/EVs/stats to back-calculate.
iv_basics.max_iv_impact: At Lv 100, a 31 IV stat is +31 over a 0 IV stat. For HP, that's +31 HP. Significant.
iv_basics.hidden_power: The type and power of Hidden Power are determined entirely by IVs. Can range from any type and 30-70 power. Used to give coverage to Pokemon (e.g., Hidden Power Fire on a Grass-type vs Steel).

ev_basics.what_are_evs: Effort Values - earned by defeating Pokemon. Each species gives specific EV yields. Max 510 EVs total per Pokemon, max 252 per stat (rounds to 255 with no benefit past 252). Every 4 EVs in a stat = +1 to that stat at Lv 100.
ev_basics.vitamins: HP Up (HP), Protein (Atk), Iron (Def), Calcium (SpA), Zinc (SpD), Carbos (Speed) each give +10 EVs. Cap at 100 EVs per stat from vitamins; rest must come from battle.
ev_basics.ev_reset_berries: Pomeg (-HP), Kelpsy (-Atk), Qualot (-Def), Hondew (-SpA), Grepa (-SpD), Tamato (-Spe) each remove 10 EVs. Useful to fix a mistrained Pokemon.
ev_basics.macho_brace_pokerus: Macho Brace doubles EV gain (halves Speed). Pokerus (rare wild status virus) ALSO doubles EVs and stacks with Macho Brace for 4x. Pokerus spreads between adjacent party members.

ev_training_spots_emerald.hp: Marill on Route 102 (1 HP EV) or Wailmer on Route 103 (1 HP EV). For 2 HP: Wynaut hatched from Lavaridge egg.
ev_training_spots_emerald.attack: Mightyena/Poochyena on Route 120 (2 Atk). Or Vigoroth in Petalburg Woods (2 Atk).
ev_training_spots_emerald.defense: Geodude on Granite Cave/Victory Road (1 Def). Trapinch in the desert (1 Def).
ev_training_spots_emerald.special_attack: Tentacool on water routes (1 SpA), or Gulpin (1 SpA) on Route 110.
ev_training_spots_emerald.special_defense: Tentacool/Tentacruel give SpD + SpA. For pure SpD: Roselia (2 SpA actually) - cleanest pure SpD is Spheal (1 SpD).
ev_training_spots_emerald.speed: Zubat in caves (1 Spe), Magikarp from fishing (1 Spe), or Electrode from New Mauville (2 Spe).

breeding_iv_ev.tip: Emerald's EV training is grindy. Always equip Macho Brace, infect your team with Pokerus, and head to a known EV spot. Use Repels matched to the species' level so you only encounter one Pokemon type.

tips: Choose Mudkip if it's your first Emerald playthrough - easiest run by far
Do the bag-grab tutorial in Petalburg Woods - free Potions
Catch a Wurmple on Route 101 (free Bug coverage; evolves into Beautifly or Dustox at random)
Talk to your dad in Petalburg Gym - he gives you HM01-related dialogue but the badge comes later
Catch Shroomish in Petalburg Woods - it evolves into Breloom and learns Mach Punch (priority Fighting move) at Lv 23. Insane against Roxanne and Norman.
Battle every trainer for EXP - Emerald's EXP curve is steep early
Buy 5-10 Potions and 3-5 Antidotes before Petalburg Woods (Poison Sting Wurmples are everywhere)
Save your money - Poke Balls cost 200 each but the Devon Goods sidequest gives you 5 free Premier Balls
Catch Aron in Granite Cave (1F) - it evolves into Lairon (Lv 32) then Aggron (Lv 42). Massive late-game Steel/Rock wall.
Talk to Steven in the deepest part of Granite Cave - he gives you TM47 Steel Wing for the Devon Goods. Steven is the OG champion (Wallace took over for Emerald) and is foreshadowed throughout.
Don't waste Repels in Granite Cave - the Aron encounter rate is decent and you want it
Sail back to Slateport to deliver the Devon Goods - this opens up Cycling Road

Get the Mach Bike or Acro Bike at Rydel's Cycles in Mauville. Mach Bike is needed for Mirage Tower (Anorith/Lileep) and Sky Pillar; Acro Bike for some Secret Base spots and Trick House. You can swap anytime by returning to Rydel.
Catch Electrike on Route 110 - evolves into Manectric, one of the best Electric types in Emerald
Slot machine the Mauville Game Corner if you're patient - TM24 Thunderbolt for 4000 Coins is a game-changer
Wattson's Dynamo Badge unlocks Rock Smash - go back and Rock Smash boulders for items, Geodude, and Nosepass

Mirage Tower in the desert (need Mach Bike + Go-Goggles) - choose ONE: Root Fossil (Lileep/Cradily) or Claw Fossil (Anorith/Armaldo). The other crumbles into the sand forever. Pick Anorith if you're light on a Rock-type, Lileep if you want a tanky Grass/Rock.
After climbing Mt. Chimney, you fight Team Magma/Aqua at the summit - heal first
Lavaridge gives you a free Wynaut egg from the old woman near the hot springs - Wobbuffet has utility in doubles
Always use the Hot Springs - they fully heal your team for free (faster than Pokemon Center for grinders)

S_tier.justification: Best starter, only one weakness (4x Grass), incredible bulk and movepool, solos most gyms.
Pseudo-legendary with 600 BST. Bagon caught in Meteor Falls (post-7th badge). Late but world-ending.
Pseudo-legendary; Beldum is a gift from Steven post-game. Insane bulk, Meteor Mash sweeps Frontier.
Best dual-STAB starter. Sky Uppercut + Blaze Kick covers most of Hoenn.
Lightning Rod ability, fast, learns Thunder/Thunderbolt. Solos Winona and Wallace.

A_tier.justification: Mach Punch priority, Effect Spore (status on contact), Sky Uppercut. Beats Roxanne, Norman, Sidney solo.
120 base Speed, Leaf Blade is the best physical Grass move in the game.
Trapinch in the desert, evolves to Vibrava (Lv 35) and Flygon (Lv 45). Levitate immunity to Ground, great offensive typing.
Sturdy/Rock Head ability, can spam Double-Edge with no recoil. Massive Defense.
Ralts on Route 102 is rare but evolves at Lv 20 (Kirlia) and Lv 30 (Gardevoir). Calm Mind + Psychic + Thunderbolt + Ice Punch is end-game tier.
Massive HP, Thick Fat ability, Vital Throw + Brick Break. Hard counter to Sidney and Glacia.
Taillow caught Route 104 day 1. Guts ability + Toxic Orb + Facade is a Frontier staple. Speedy revenge killer.
Zubat -> Golbat -> Crobat (high friendship). Mean Look catches roaming Latios/Latias.
Marvel Scale ability + Recover + Ice Beam. Tedious to evolve (Feebas -> Beauty contest stat) but elite once obtained.

B_tier.justification: Spheal in Shoal Cave evolves to Sealeo (Lv 32) and Walrein (Lv 44). Thick Fat Ice attacker that survives Fire moves.
Numel from Route 112. Magnitude + Flamethrower covers a lot, but 4x Water weak.
Belly Drum + Extreme Speed + Pickup ability for free items. Best HM slave with Cut/Surf/Strength/Dig support.
Route 120 (rare). Super Luck + Slash for crit fishing, Swords Dance + Aerial Ace devastates Phoebe.
Liquid Ooze prevents drain heals against you. Sludge Bomb + Surf + Ice Beam coverage.
Sturdy/Magnet Pull, traps Steels. Trades have it evolve to Magnezone in later gens but in Emerald it's Magneton-locked.

honorable_mentions.justification: Lotad on Route 102 (rare). Rain Dish + Swift Swim. Unique typing only weak to Bug/Flying/Poison.
Insane stats but Truant ability halves uptime. Only viable in Frontier with Skill Swap support.
Swablu evolves at Lv 35. Bulky Dragon with Dragon Dance + Earthquake.

battle_frontier.location: Artificial island reachable by ship from Lilycove (after Hall of Fame entry)
battle_frontier.currency: Battle Points (BP) earned from win streaks

facilities.name: Battle Tower
facilities.format: Choose 3 Pokemon, win 7 battles in a row, repeat
facilities.level: Lv 50 (with auto-level option) or Open Lv 100
facilities.tips: Use a balanced trio: physical wall, special wall, sweeper. Ban yourself from gimmicks.

facilities.name: Battle Dome
facilities.gimmick: You can scout enemy Pokemon's stats and moves before battle.
facilities.tips: Use Choice Band attackers - the format favors quick OHKOs.

facilities.name: Battle Palace
facilities.format: 3v3 single, but YOU don't pick moves - your Pokemon's Nature decides what they do each turn
facilities.gimmick: Aggressive natures attack, defensive ones heal/buff. RNG-heavy.
facilities.tips: Use Adamant/Modest natures with high BP single-purpose movesets.

facilities.name: Battle Arena
facilities.format: 1v1 with a 3-turn judging system - if both Pokemon are alive after 3 turns, judges decide winner by Mind/Skill/Body scores
facilities.gimmick: Switching is banned. Status moves are penalized.
facilities.tips: Use offensive natures and STAB moves only. Slaking shines here (Truant doesn't dock score).

facilities.name: Battle Factory
facilities.format: Rental Pokemon - you get a random pool, choose 3, then can swap with defeated opponents' Pokemon after each round
facilities.gimmick: You don't use your own Pokemon at all.
facilities.tips: Pick balanced typing on round 1. Always swap into a Pokemon that covers your previous loss.

facilities.name: Battle Pike
facilities.format: Choose a path through 14 rooms, each containing trainers, wild Pokemon, status traps, or NPCs
facilities.gimmick: Lottery-style; you pick from 3 doors blind. Avoid status floors with Lum Berry.
facilities.tips: Use a tank with Recover/Rest. Bring Lum Berries on every Pokemon.

facilities.name: Battle Pyramid
facilities.format: 7-floor dark maze with wild Pokemon, trainers, and items. Must find the boss on each floor with limited Flash range.
facilities.gimmick: Held items don't carry between floors - you find new ones in the maze.
facilities.tips: Use Pokemon with high HP and self-recovery (Leftovers found in maze). Map memorization helps.

post_game.no_sevii_islands: Note: Emerald does NOT have Sevii Islands - that's a FireRed/LeafGreen feature. Emerald's post-game equivalents are the Battle Frontier, Trainer Hill, and the legendary trio quest (Regis, roaming Latios/Latias, weather trio).
post_game.trainer_hill: Located near the Mauville-Verdanturf cable car route. 4-floor trainer rush - clear all rooms in the fastest time. The default has 3 fixed trainer themes (Variety, Unique, Strong). Records are saved as time stamps.

rematches.gym_leaders: Gym Leader rematches are NOT in vanilla Emerald (they're in HeartGold/SoulSilver). Hoenn rematches were added retroactively in Omega Ruby/Alpha Sapphire.
rematches.regular_trainers: Trainers across Hoenn give you their PokeNav numbers. Call them or wait for them to call you - they'll offer rematches with stronger teams. The strongest rematches are post-Hall of Fame and give massive EXP.

post_game_legendaries: Rayquaza (Sky Pillar - returns post-game if defeated)
Latios or Latias (roaming, the other via Eon Ticket)
post_game.steven_rematch: After becoming Champion, talk to Steven Stone in his Mossdeep house - he gives you a Beldum (eventual Metagross). He also has secret rematch dialogue in his house referencing Hoenn lore.
completion_goals: Catch all 386 Pokemon (requires trades with FireRed/LeafGreen and Ruby/Sapphire)
Earn all 14 Battle Frontier Symbols (Silver and Gold)
Beat Trainer Hill in record time

bikes.overview: Two bikes exist in Emerald: the Mach Bike (raw speed, slope climbing) and the Acro Bike (tricks, white rails, hopping tiles). You can only carry one at a time, but Rydel will swap them at no cost as often as you want.

acquisition.when: Free as soon as you reach Mauville (after the 2nd badge from Brawly). Pick either bike on first visit.
acquisition.swap_rules: Talk to Rydel any time to exchange bikes. No item, no cost, no cooldown.

mach_bike.summary: Pure speed. Doubles your movement and lets you climb dirt slopes that walking cannot.
abilities: Climbs muddy/dirt slopes at full speed
Breaks cracked floors when ridden over them at full speed (fall through to the floor below)
Outruns the Pacifidlog tide currents on Route 132/133/134
mach_bike.controls: Hold a directional button to accelerate. Speed builds over a few tiles - you must commit to a straight line before slope climbs and crack-floor jumps.
mach_bike.best_for: Story progression, dungeon climbs, escape from currents

acro_bike.summary: Trick bike. Slower than Mach but unlocks bunny hops, wheelies, and white-rail traversal.
abilities: Bunny Hop: tap and hold B with no direction - hops in place. Used to cross Hopping Tiles (yellow circle pads) in Trick House and on the path to the Berry Master.
Wheelie Hop: hold B then tap a direction - hops one tile in that direction without sliding. Used to cross gap tiles.
Wheelie: hold B and move - rides on the back wheel. Required to cross white-bar rails (thin white horizontal lines on the field) and to cross cracked floors WITHOUT breaking them in Sky Pillar.
Side Hop: tap B + perpendicular direction while on a rail - jump on/off rails
acro_bike.controls: B button is your trick modifier. Single tap = hop, hold = wheelie, B + direction = directional hop.
acro_bike.best_for: Trick House late puzzles, Sky Pillar (Rayquaza), Safari Zone NE/NW expansions, several Secret Base entrances, Berry Master path

where_each_bike_is_required.location: Mt. Chimney ash slope (Route 112 / Jagged Pass approach)
where_each_bike_is_required.action: Climb the soft-ash slope from Fiery Path side up to Lavaridge approach. Walking is rejected.
Series of dirt slopes inside the lava-filled cave; cannot be navigated without Mach acceleration.
Cracked floors inside the tower break under speed - intentional in this case, as you need to drop to lower floors to grab the Root/Claw Fossil.
The cracked floors here will break and drop you if you ride fast. Use Acro Wheelie to traverse them slowly without breaking. Mach Bike will trap you on lower floors.
Bunny hop on yellow circular pads to advance through the puzzle.
Wheelie across the white rails to reach the Berry Master and his rare-berry-giving wife.
Hopping Tiles gate entry to the new areas added after Hall of Fame entry.
Several connecting log bridges between houses use white rails; Acro Bike makes traversal faster.
Many tree-and-rail Secret Base entry tiles require wheelie traversal.
Can't outrun water currents on the bike, but on the bridge sections leading in/out you'll want Mach Bike speed.

field_tricks.wheelie_howto: Acro Bike only. Hold B (no direction) until your character pops to one wheel, then push a direction to roll. Stay on the rear wheel to traverse white rails and Sky Pillar's cracked floors safely.
field_tricks.bunny_hop_howto: Acro Bike only. Tap-and-hold B with no direction; you'll bounce in place. Required for yellow Hopping Tiles.
field_tricks.directional_hop_howto: Acro Bike only. Press B then immediately tap a direction - the hop covers one tile in that direction without inertia.
field_tricks.slope_slide: Mach Bike on dirt slopes will climb. Walking down a slope works either way; walking UP a slope is impossible without the Mach Bike.

bikes.quick_swap_tip: Always swap to whichever bike you need before entering a long dungeon. Rydel's is centrally located in Mauville and free - no reason to suffer through Sky Pillar with a Mach Bike or Magma Hideout with an Acro.
bikes.exclusive_emerald_note: Emerald is the only Gen 3 game where you can own both bikes (one at a time, free swaps) without needing to find Rydel's missing bike like in Ruby/Sapphire.

evolution_mechanics.overview: Gen 3 Emerald supports level-up, stone, trade, friendship, beauty (contest stat), and conditional evolutions. Many evolutions can be intentionally delayed or cancelled, with real movepool consequences.

evolution_types.description: Hits a target level and evolves automatically at end of battle. Most common.
Use a stone item from the bag. Instant, no level requirement (above species minimum).
Trade the Pokemon over a link cable. Evolves on receipt by the new trainer. NOT possible single-cart - you need a partner Game Boy/cable.
Level up while friendship value is >= 220 (out of 255). Soothe Bell speeds this up.
examples: Azurill -> Marill (low friendship route, despite name)

evolution_types.description: Level up while contest Beauty stat is sufficiently high. Unique to Feebas in Emerald.
Evolution outcome is determined by the Pokemon's hidden Personality Value (PV), set at encounter/hatch and never changed.
Levels up but only if certain extra conditions are met.
examples: Nincada -> Ninjask at Lv 20 (and Shedinja appears in your party box if you have an empty slot and a spare Pokeball)

cancelling_evolution.how: When the evolution animation starts, press and hold the B button until the 'Huh?' message displays. The Pokemon stays in its current form.
cancelling_evolution.permanent: No - it just delays. The next time it levels up, the prompt fires again.
why_cancel_examples: Hitmonchan and Hitmonlee can keep learning Tyrogue's pre-evo moves longer (not relevant in Emerald natively)
Slowpoke learning Slack Off at Lv 36 - Slowbro learns it later
Vibrava learns Dragonbreath at Lv 35 - Flygon evolves at Lv 45 and DOESN'T relearn it via level-up, so let Vibrava hit 35 first
Gloom learns Petal Dance and Sludge Bomb that Vileplume cannot learn naturally - delay the Leaf Stone if you want both
Eevee can never learn Bite or Take Down once evolved - keep it as Eevee until you have what you want
Kadabra learns Recover, Future Sight, Trick - Alakazam learns the same set, so trading early is fine

evolution_delays_movepool.principle: Most evolved forms learn moves later - or never - compared to their pre-evolutions. Hold off on stones and trade evolutions until you've gotten the moves you want.

specific_emerald_examples.delay_reason: Gloom learns Petal Dance (Lv 39) and Moonlight (Lv 49). Vileplume's level-up pool stops at Aromatherapy. Waiting on the Leaf Stone is correct.
Vibrava learns Dragon Breath at Lv 35; Flygon does NOT learn this naturally (only via tutor in later games). Hit Lv 35 as Vibrava before evolving at Lv 45.
Eevee learns Bite (Lv 16), Take Down (Lv 30), and Last Resort isn't in Gen 3. Eeveelutions get type-specific elemental attacks but lose the Normal moves. Decide your build before stoning.
Both learn the same level-up set (Calm Mind, Future Sight, Hypnosis line). Evolve at Lv 30 freely.
Nuzleaf learns Extrasensory at Lv 39 by level-up; Shiftry doesn't level-up learn this. Don't use the Leaf Stone before Lv 39.
Pikachu learns Thunder at Lv 50; Raichu does NOT learn Thunder by level-up. If you don't have TM25, keep Pikachu until Lv 50.
Magikarp's pool is famously useless (Splash, Tackle, Flail) - evolve immediately at Lv 20. The exception that proves the rule.
Dragonair learns Outrage at Lv 51 vs Dragonite at Lv 61 - delay evolution for Outrage.

wurmple_branching.myth: Many players believe Wurmple's evolution is rolled when it evolves. False.
wurmple_branching.truth: Wurmple's evolution path is determined by its Personality Value (PV), a 32-bit number set when the Wurmple is generated (encountered or hatched). Specifically: ((PV >> 16) % 10) - if 0-4, it becomes Silcoon -> Beautifly. If 5-9, Cascoon -> Dustox.
wurmple_branching.implication: If you save before evolving and reset, you'll always get the same outcome - the Wurmple was 'fated' from capture. To get the other branch, catch a different Wurmple.
wurmple_branching.tip: Hatch eggs from a Wurmple parent and check at Lv 7 evolution: deterministic but indistinguishable until the cocoon stage.

not_in_emerald: Espeon - friendship + day, only obtainable in Gen 2 (HeartGold/SoulSilver) or via XD: Gale of Darkness import
Umbreon - friendship + night, same restriction as Espeon
eevee_in_emerald.where_to_get_eevee: Eevee is NOT obtainable in vanilla Emerald wild. Trade from Pokemon FireRed/LeafGreen (Celadon Game Corner reward, 4900 coins) or Pokemon XD/Colosseum.
eevee_in_emerald.tip: If you have an Eevee from FRLG, use a Luxury Ball + Soothe Bell to speed friendship for the future Espeon/Umbreon evolutions when transferred forward.

trade_evolutions.single_cart_problem: Without a second GBA + Emerald/Ruby/Sapphire/FireRed/LeafGreen and a link cable, you cannot evolve trade Pokemon. They will sit in their pre-evolved form forever.
trade_evos_in_hoenn_dex: Onix -> Steelix (trade holding Metal Coat, only possible after FRLG/Colosseum import; not native to Emerald wild)
Scyther -> Scizor (trade holding Metal Coat, also not native)
Clamperl -> Huntail (trade holding Deep Sea Tooth)
Clamperl -> Gorebyss (trade holding Deep Sea Scale)
trade_evolutions.tip: Use a wireless or link adapter even with a flash cart on a real GBA - the easiest single-player trade evolution path is two GBAs and a link cable, even if both carts are yours.

friendship_evolutions_in_hoenn.tip: Catch Zubat in Granite Cave / Mt. Pyre / Meteor Falls. Golbat at Lv 22. Soothe Bell + Luxury Ball cuts time roughly in half.
Pichu only obtainable by breeding Pikachu (hold Light Ball for the egg-move boost). Pikachu itself is rare in Safari Zone.
Misleadingly NOT a low-friendship evolution despite Azurill's nervous flavor text. Hatch from Marill or Azumarill egg.
Not native to Hoenn wild; hatch from a Jigglypuff/Wigglytuff egg if traded in.
Same - not Hoenn-native; baby form bred from Clefairy/Clefable.

locations: Fiery Path (use Rock Smash on hidden rock)
Pacifidlog Town gift NPC after Mystery Event
Mossdeep Space Center NPC (gift after talking to scientists)
NOT sold in Lilycove - more limited supply
Route 114 (gift from Lanette's house brother / verify_in_game)

feebas_milotic_walkthrough.step_1_find_feebas: Feebas spawns on 6 random tiles out of 600 on Route 119. Tile selection is tied to your Trainer ID - it's static for one save file. Use the Super Rod and fish tile-by-tile until you hook one.
feebas_milotic_walkthrough.step_2_max_beauty: Feed Dry-flavored Pokeblocks until Beauty stat caps. Best berries: Pamtre Berry (the highest single-flavor Dry yield). Use a Berry Blender with 3 NPCs (Pokenav match call) for higher tier blocks.
feebas_milotic_walkthrough.step_3_level_up: Once Beauty is maxed, Feebas evolves into Milotic on its next level-up. Even one EXP candy/Rare Candy will trigger.
feebas_milotic_walkthrough.tip: Pokeblock quality (level) matters - aim for Lv 23+ blocks. The NPC parents in Slateport/Lilycove offer free quick blends if you talk to them.

should_i_evolve_decision_framework.guidance: If the pre-evolution learns a critical move at a higher level, delay. (See evolution_delays_movepool.)
Most evolutions front-load 30-50 BST gain. If you're stuck losing matchups, evolving usually fixes things faster than grinding.

Combusken -> Blaziken keeps Fire/Fighting. Marshtomp -> Swampert keeps Water/Ground. But Azurill -> Marill swaps to Water type. Nincada becomes Ninjask Bug/Flying. Wurmple branches by PV. Plan typing intentionally.
Some evolved forms have a slower EXP table. Sceptile and Blaziken use 'Slow' curve - evolving doesn't help here, but the BST jump usually outweighs it.
Stones consume the item. Save before applying - especially for Eevee (only one Eevee in Emerald in most runs).

rare_candy_vs_natural.overview: Rare Candy hands you a level for free but yields ZERO EVs. The Pokemon's stats trail equivalent natural-leveled team members until EVs catch up. Knowing when to use Rare Candy is the difference between a competitive Pokemon and a deficit Pokemon.
rare_candy_vs_natural.what_rare_candy_does: Adds exactly enough EXP to ding the next level. Ignores EXP-table tier. Cannot be used at Lv 100. Triggers normal evolution checks (level + friendship/Beauty conditions).
rare_candy_vs_natural.what_rare_candy_doesnt_do: Does NOT grant EVs. Does NOT increase friendship. Does NOT trigger Pokerus or Macho Brace effects (no battle = no EVs).
rare_candy_vs_natural.the_252_510_cap: Each Pokemon gets 510 total EVs and 252 max per stat (any 253-255 are wasted with no benefit). 252 EVs in a stat = +63 to that stat at Lv 100 (not Lv 50 - it's halved).

natural_vs_candied_example.scenario: Two identical Adamant Salamence at Lv 50, one fully EV-trained (252 Atk / 252 Spe / 4 HP) and the other Rare-Candied with no EVs.
natural_vs_candied_example.stat_difference_atk: EV-trained: 31 Atk EVs at Lv 50 = +15 stat. Candied: 0 EV = +0. Real fight result: ~12% higher damage on every hit.
natural_vs_candied_example.stat_difference_spe: Same logic: candied Salamence is 7 points slower at Lv 50, getting outsped by Jolly Ttar mirrors.
natural_vs_candied_example.lesson: Always EV train BEFORE Rare-Candying for level.

when_to_use_rare_candy: After fully EV-training and you need to push from Lv 49 to Lv 50 for the Battle Frontier auto-level cap (Frontier scales DOWN to 50 but does NOT scale UP, so Lv 50 is the sweet spot)
On a Pokemon you've capped 510 EVs already - the candy is pure level gain
To break a 'Pokemon won't obey' wall after a new badge - one or two candies to clear the obedience threshold and avoid retrained-from-trade headaches
On rentals/gift Pokemon you don't care about EV-optimizing (story-mode plays, casual runs)
Last few levels (Lv 95-100) when EV gain plateaus at 510 anyway - candies skip endgame grind
when_NOT_to_use_rare_candy: While EV training - every level gained from candy is a level you're not gaining EVs at
On a Pokemon you plan to Frontier-rent at Lv 100 (Frontier auto-levels to 50; level beyond that is wasted)
Before knowing your Nature - evolutions and stat curves at low levels reveal Nature; candy past it and you've burned info
On Pokemon you intend to breed - parents pass IVs and held-item quirks regardless of level; over-leveling parents is wasted candy

rare_candy_locations_emerald.notes: Use the Item Finder in the desert; one candy is buried.
Visible item in the back wall area.
Visible item near the entrance pool.
Visible on the upper outdoor floor before the summit.
Drop through cracked floor to reach it before fossil chamber.

rare_candy_locations_emerald.location: Magma/Aqua Hideout (one each, version dependent on plot path)
rare_candy_locations_emerald.notes: After clearing the underwater base storyline.

rare_candy_locations_emerald.location: Lilycove Lottery (1st prize: Master Ball, 2nd: PP-Up, 3rd: Exp Share, 4th: PP-Up, 5th: Rare Candy)
rare_candy_locations_emerald.notes: Match 5 trainer-ID digits for top prize; 2 digits for the candy.
Rare drop at high levels (Lv 91-100 has ~1% Rare Candy chance per battle ended).
Candy can be bought for BP at the Frontier shop (price: 48 BP, verify_in_game).

day_care_xp_glitch.what: Pokemon left at the Day Care gain 1 EXP per step you take. They do NOT gain EVs from steps. Effectively, Day Care steps are slow Rare Candy.
day_care_xp_glitch.abc_corner_select_glitch: An old technique with the Daycare's box-name/screen interactions claims to skip step counts. This is mostly a Gen 1/2 bug; in Gen 3 Emerald, the verified Day Care behavior is straight 1 EXP / step. Don't expect a duplication exploit.
day_care_xp_glitch.tip: Drop a low-level Pokemon at the Day Care, ride your Mach Bike in the Pacifidlog current loop or the Verdanturf-Mauville cable car loop overnight - tens of thousands of steps stack up. EVs are still zero, so plan accordingly.
day_care_xp_glitch.verify_in_game: Specific corner-and-select tricks vary by ROM revision; treat as anecdotal.

lucky_egg_in_emerald.drop_source: Held by wild Chansey, 5% chance. Chansey appears in Route 113 ash grass, possibly Safari Zone (verify_in_game) - in Hoenn it is generally rare without trading.
lucky_egg_in_emerald.how_to_use: Held item; the holder gains 1.5x EXP per battle. Stack with EXP-Share (Gen 3 EXP Share splits the pool but the holder keeps Lucky Egg multiplier).
lucky_egg_in_emerald.thief_strategy: Bring a Pokemon with the move Thief (TM46) and a Pokemon NOT holding an item. Thief into Chansey - 5% gives a Lucky Egg. Repeat as needed; one is usually enough.

vitamins.cap: Each vitamin caps that stat's EVs at 100. Past 100, vitamins fail with 'It won't have any effect!' message. Beyond 100 must come from battle EV yield.
vitamins.cost: 9,800 each at Mauville City and Slateport City Pokemarts. Department Store rooftop sometimes runs sales.
vitamins.math: Going from 0 to 100 EVs in a stat: 10 vitamins x 9,800 = 98,000. Then 152 EVs more from battle to hit 252. Saves dozens of battles per stat.
vitamins.stack_with_macho_brace_pokerus: Vitamins ARE NOT multiplied by Macho Brace or Pokerus. Those only multiply battle EV yield.

all_six.verify_in_game: Tamato boost stat varies by source

ev_reset_berries.tip: Save reset berries for mistrained Pokemon. They also raise contest stats - dual purpose with Feebas/Milotic projects.

rare_candy_vs_natural.tldr: Battle and EV-train first. THEN Rare Candy to hit your target level. Vitamins are the fast-forward button for the first 100 EVs per stat. Reset berries fix mistakes.

iv_ev_deep.overview: Deeper than breeding_iv_ev. Covers the math, the heritage rules, the Pokerus virus, the mechanical limits, and how to verify IVs in Gen 3 (where there's no IV-judge dialogue like later gens).

ivs_explained.range: 0 to 31 per stat. Six stats: HP, Atk, Def, SpA, SpD, Spe.
ivs_explained.set_when: Generated at the moment of encounter (wild) or hatching (egg) or gift (NPC). NEVER changes after.
ivs_explained.stat_impact_lv50: Per IV point: +0.5 stat at Lv 50 (so 31 IV = +15 stat over 0 IV). HP differs - it's roughly +0.5 HP per IV at Lv 50.
ivs_explained.stat_impact_lv100: Per IV point: +1 stat at Lv 100. So 31 IV = +31 stat over 0 IV.
ivs_explained.max_combination: Perfect 31/31/31/31/31/31 is the goal. With Adamant nature on a physical attacker: -10% SpA hidden. With Modest on a special attacker: -10% Atk.

hidden_power_calculation.what_determines_type: All six IVs' parities (even vs odd). Specifically: ((HP_odd + 2*Atk_odd + 4*Def_odd + 8*Spe_odd + 16*SpA_odd + 32*SpD_odd) * 15 / 63), floored. The result 0-15 maps to a type (Fighting=0, Flying=1, ... Dark=15).
hidden_power_calculation.what_determines_power: Floor((sum of IV components) / 8 * 40 + 30). Where each stat contributes IF its IV mod 4 is >= 2. Power range: 30 to 70.

common_hp_examples.ivs: 31/30/31/31/30/31 (-2 Atk, -2 SpD - common 'HP Ice' for Special attackers)

hidden_power_calculation.tip: If you're breeding for HP Ice on a special sweeper (Manectric, Gardevoir): target 31/x/31/31/31/31 with Atk dropped 1 (HP Ice 70). Atk drop also helps because Foul Play's coming gens use it - irrelevant in Gen 3 but harmless.

iv_breeding_heritage_gen3.rule: When an egg hatches, exactly 3 random IVs out of 6 are inherited from the parents. The other 3 are rolled randomly 0-31.
iv_breeding_heritage_gen3.heritage_method: Step 1: Roll which 3 stats inherit (random selection). Step 2: For each inherited stat, randomly pick which parent (mom or dad) supplied it. Step 3: That parent's IV in that stat is copied exactly to the egg.
iv_breeding_heritage_gen3.destiny_knot_note: Destiny Knot (boosts to 5 inherited IVs) is a Gen 6+ feature. NOT in Emerald. Gen 3 is stuck at 3 inherited.
iv_breeding_heritage_gen3.implication: Breeding for 6x31 IVs in Gen 3 takes drastically more eggs than in Gen 6+. Realistic Gen 3 goal: 4-5 perfect IVs after several hundred eggs.
iv_breeding_heritage_gen3.everstone_nature: Female parent with Everstone passes Nature 50% of the time. Independent of IV inheritance.

power_items_dont_exist_gen3.what_are_power_items: Macho Brace exists in Gen 3 but doesn't pass IVs. Gen 4 introduced Power Bracer/Power Belt/Power Anklet/Power Lens/Power Band/Power Weight, which guarantee one IV inheritance from the holder.
power_items_dont_exist_gen3.implication_for_emerald: Gen 3 IV breeding is RNG-only (3 random IVs). No way to lock specific IVs from a parent. Just breed many, many eggs and check stats post-hatch.

hp.tip: Wild Marill at Lv 13-15. Stack Macho Brace + Pokerus = 8 HP per kill. 32 kills to max.
Slow grind unless you want HP/Spe split.

atk.tip: Adjacent to weather institute. Highest density Atk EV in mid-game.
Use Pelipper if you also want some Spe.

def.location: Granite Cave / Victory Road / Rusturf Tunnel
def.tip: Common; ride Repels to skip Zubat.
Higher level = more EXP per Def kill.
Lower spawn rate; mostly ride Geodude.

spa.tip: Fastest SpA grind in the game with Pokerus + Brace = 4 per kill.
Rare encounter; if you find one, it's the best SpA yield in the area.

spd.tip: Common misconception - Roselia gives SpA. Spheal is the SpD answer.

spe.location: Granite Cave / Mt. Pyre / Meteor Falls

spe.tip: Slow grind unless you want easy XP too.
Highest Spe yield. Watch for Self-Destruct - bring a Damp partner or one-shot it.

macho_brace.effect: Doubles EV gain from battles (does NOT affect vitamins). Halves the holder's Speed in battle (only during the battle, not the EV calc).
macho_brace.stack_with_pokerus: Macho Brace + Pokerus = 4x EV gain. Tentacool gives 4 SpA per kill instead of 1.
macho_brace.where_to_get: Free gift from an NPC in Slateport City - inside the contest hall area NPC near the market. Just one - infect your team-leader Pokemon and rotate the brace.
macho_brace.tip: Always brace your training Pokemon. Speed cut doesn't matter when you're killing Lv 5 Marills.

pokerus.what_is_it: A 'virus' status that doubles EVs gained while infected. Visible in summary screen with a smiley-face indicator and 'Pokerus' note.
pokerus.infection_rate: Approximately 1 in 21,845 wild encounters (slightly higher with chained encounters or RNG quirks - varies by ROM).
pokerus.spread: Pokerus spreads to adjacent party members each time the infected Pokemon's holder takes a step. Position the infected Pokemon next to teammates who need it.
pokerus.duration: Active for 1-4 days (in-game time, real RTC). After it expires, the Pokemon shows a face indicator (yellow dot) but EV bonus is gone.
pokerus.preserve: Store an infected Pokemon in the PC. Time does not pass for boxed Pokemon. You can always pull it out, infect a fresh team, and re-store.
pokerus.tip: Pokerus is the single biggest EV-training accelerator in the game. Hunt for it on Routes 118-119 if your save doesn't have it. Trade with friends - if any has it, infection passes through trades.

ev_spread_principles.common_spread: 252 / 252 / 4 - max two stats, 4 leftover. Standard offensive spread (252 Atk / 252 Spe / 4 HP for physical sweepers, etc.).
ev_spread_principles.why_252: EVs convert at 4 EV : 1 stat point. 252 / 4 = 63 stat points (the maximum benefit since 253-255 round down).
ev_spread_principles.why_4_leftover: Putting 4 in a third stat = +1 stat. Better than 0; better than wasting 4 in an already-maxed stat.
ev_spread_principles.bulky_spread: 252 HP / 252 Def / 4 SpD or 252 HP / 4 Def / 252 SpD - choose the defense to invest in based on threats.
ev_spread_principles.mixed_attacker: Less common: 128 Atk / 128 SpA / 252 Spe. Half-baked - usually pick one offense and dump the other in HP.

effort_ribbon.what: A visible ribbon awarded by an NPC in Slateport (next to the trainer school, north area NPC, verify_in_game). She inspects your Pokemon and gives the Effort Ribbon if EVs total exactly 510.
effort_ribbon.purpose: Visual confirmation. There is no in-game stat reader, so this ribbon is the closest you get to 'EV training complete' acknowledgment. Cosmetic only - no battle effect.

checking_ivs_without_calculator.battle_frontier_iv_judge: There is NO Battle-Frontier IV-judge NPC in Gen 3 Emerald. This was added in HeartGold/SoulSilver and later. To 'judge' an IV, you must use stat calculations.
checking_ivs_without_calculator.the_lv50_test: Calculate the expected stat at Lv 50 with 0 EVs. Compare to your Pokemon's actual stat. The delta gives the IV.
checking_ivs_without_calculator.stat_formula_lv50: (((2 * Base + IV + EV/4) * Lv) / 100) + 5, then multiply by Nature.
checking_ivs_without_calculator.online_calc_recommendation: Smogon Stat Calculator or psypoke IV calc. Input level, nature, EVs, stat values, get IV ranges.

natures_25.summary: 25 natures. 5 are 'neutral' (no stat effect): Hardy, Docile, Serious, Bashful, Quirky. The other 20 boost one stat 10% and reduce another 10%.
natures_25.synchronize_breeding: Female holding Everstone has 50% nature pass-down to offspring (best Gen 3 method). Synchronize ability on a lead Pokemon (Ralts/Kirlia/Gardevoir, also Abra) sets the wild encounter to YOUR Synchronize-Pokemon's nature 50% of the time. Useful for legendary capture.
natures_25.tip: Pair Everstone breeding with a Synchronize Ralts in your party for catching Latios/Latias/Regis with desired natures.

type_chart_quirks.overview: Gen 3 type chart. Fairy type does not exist (added Gen 6). Steel resists Ghost and Dark. Ghost is immune to Normal and Fighting. Dark is immune to Psychic.
type_chart_quirks.type_count: 17 types in Gen 3: Normal, Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Ice, Fighting, Poison, Ground, Flying, Psychic, Bug, Rock, Ghost, Dragon, Dark, Steel.

stab_explained.what: Same Type Attack Bonus. When a Pokemon uses a move matching its own type, damage is multiplied by 1.5x.
stab_explained.dual_type_stab: Dual-type Pokemon (e.g., Salamence Dragon/Flying) get STAB on both Dragon and Flying moves.
stab_explained.tip: STAB matters more in Emerald than later gens because move BPs are lower across the board. A 95-BP STAB Surf hits like 142.5 BP.

dual_type_calculation.rule: Type effectiveness multiplies. A 2x weakness on one type and a 2x weakness on the other = 4x. A 2x weakness and a 0.5x resist = 1x neutral.
key_examples: Salamence (Dragon/Flying) hit by Ice: Ice is 2x on Dragon, 2x on Flying = 4x damage
Swampert (Water/Ground) hit by Grass: 2x on Water, 2x on Ground = 4x damage. ONLY weakness.
Bronzong (Steel/Psychic) hit by Fire: 2x on Steel, 0.5x on Psychic = 1x neutral
Skarmory (Steel/Flying) hit by Rock: 0.5x Steel, 2x Flying = 1x neutral. NOT 2x like pure Flying.
Gyarados (Water/Flying) hit by Electric: 2x Water, 2x Flying = 4x damage

gen3_specific_quirks.note: Changed in Gen 6 - Steel no longer resists those. In Gen 3, Steel is the most resistant defensive type (11 resistances, 1 immunity to Poison).

gen3_specific_quirks.quirk: Ghost is immune to Normal AND Fighting
gen3_specific_quirks.note: Dual immunity. Hariyama with Brick Break does ZERO damage to a Gengar. Use Crunch (Dark) or Shadow Ball (Ghost) instead.
Sableye and Sharpedo eat Psychic moves. Tate & Liza's gym is hard-countered by Sharpedo.

gen3_specific_quirks.quirk: Bug is super effective vs Dark, Psychic, Grass
gen3_specific_quirks.note: Heracross's Megahorn is a top-tier nuke vs Slowking, Gardevoir, etc. Sidney's whole team.
Standard but worth noting - 0.5x. Also Fire 0.5x against Fire/Rock/Dragon/Water.
But Levitate ability (Flygon, Gengar, Claydol) makes them immune to Ground. Magneton is Steel/Electric so it's still hit by Ground.

gen3_specific_quirks.quirk: Poison is super effective only vs Grass in Gen 3
gen3_specific_quirks.note: Used to also be SE vs Bug in Gen 1; that ended in Gen 2. Fairy doesn't exist yet, so Poison's offensive coverage is weak.

gen3_specific_quirks.quirk: Ghost is super effective vs Psychic and Ghost
Dragon is super effective ONLY vs Dragon
gen3_specific_quirks.note: No Fairy resistance yet. Outrage from Salamence walls everything that doesn't resist or immune.

type_matchup_card_npc.where: Battle Frontier Lounge - one of the Frontier-area NPCs gives gameplay-tip dialogue. Also an in-game dialogue board in Trainer Hill that summarizes type chart.
type_matchup_card_npc.verify_in_game: Specific NPC location varies by emulator/translation; the Frontier reception area has the most type-chart dialogue.

top_5_types_for_emerald_playthrough.why: Surf is mandatory anyway (HM03). Hits Fire/Rock/Ground for 2x. Mudkip/Marshtomp/Swampert dominates 5 of 8 gyms.
Earthquake (TM26) is universal coverage. Hits Fire/Electric/Poison/Rock/Steel. Swampert and Flygon thrive.
Thunderbolt (TM24) and Thunder hit Water and Flying (every late gym leader has Water/Flying). Manectric/Magneton sweep.
Ice Beam (TM13) hits Dragon/Flying/Grass/Ground for 2x. Drake's gym is solo'd by Ice. Walrein/Glalie/Lapras.
Brick Break (TM31) breaks Light Screen/Reflect AND hits Steel/Rock/Dark/Normal/Ice. Hariyama and Blaziken cover gym/Frontier.

common_type_mistakes: Using Fighting on Sableye (Sableye is Dark/Ghost, immune to Fighting from Ghost half)
Using Psychic on Sharpedo or Mightyena (Dark immunity)
Using Ground on Skarmory (Skarmory is Steel/Flying, Flying half is immune to Ground)
Using Rock on Skarmory (Skarmory is Steel, resists Rock)
Forgetting Levitate on Flygon/Gengar/Claydol when planning Earthquake spam

status_conditions.overview: Status conditions are debuffs that persist past a single move. Sleep, Paralysis, Burn, Poison, Freeze, Confusion, and Attraction. Each has different mechanics, durations, and counter-items.

sleep.effect: Pokemon cannot select moves; turns spent asleep wake up on their own.
sleep.proc_rate: Direct sleep moves vary: Hypnosis 60% acc, Spore 100% acc, Sing 55% acc, Sleep Powder 75% acc, Yawn (Slaking) 100% but 1-turn delay.
ability_immunity: Early Bird (halves sleep duration: Doduo, Houndoom)
wake_up_conditions: Take damage from a Fire move (sometimes) - actually false in Gen 3; only specific moves wake.
Use the move Snore or Sleep Talk while asleep - they work without waking
Switch out and switch back in - resets sleep counter? No, sleep counter persists.

paralysis.effect: 25% chance per turn to skip move (full paralysis), AND Speed reduced to 25% of normal in Gen 3.
paralysis.speed_multiplier_gen3: Paralyzed Pokemon's Speed stat is multiplied by 0.25 in battle. Note: this was changed to 0.5 in Gen 7; in Emerald it's a hard 1/4.
paralysis.proc_rate: Thunder Wave 100% acc but immune-gated (Ground immune in later gens, NOT in Gen 3 - in Gen 3 Thunder Wave can paralyze Ground types). Body Slam 30% on contact. Lick 30%.
ability_immunity: Limber (Glameow, Buneary line - none native to Hoenn except via trade)
paralysis.type_immunity_gen3: Electric types are NOT immune to paralysis in Gen 3 (changed in Gen 6). Only abilities like Limber prevent it.

burn.effect: 1/8 max HP damage at end of turn AND Atk stat halved (multiplied by 0.5) for the duration of the burn.
burn.proc_sources: Will-O-Wisp 75% acc, Flamethrower 10%, Fire Blast 10%, Sacred Fire 50%, Lava Plume 30% (Gen 4+ - not Emerald)
ability_immunity: Flame Body (Magmar, Magcargo - 30% to burn opponent on contact)
burn.type_immunity: Fire types are immune to burn from any source in Gen 3.
burn.halved_atk_implication: Physical attackers (Salamence, Hariyama) lose half their offensive power. Special attackers are unaffected. Burn is the best status against physical sweepers.

regular_poison.effect: 1/8 max HP damage at end of turn
regular_poison.duration: Permanent until cured. Outlasts battle (took damage during overworld walking in Gen 3 - 1 HP per 4 steps).
regular_poison.proc_sources: Poison Sting 30%, Sludge 30%, Sludge Bomb 30%, Poison Powder 75% acc

toxic_badly_poisoned.effect: Damage increases each turn: 1/16 turn 1, 2/16 turn 2, 3/16 turn 3, etc. Caps at 15/16 max HP per turn.
toxic_badly_poisoned.duration: Reverts to regular poison if Pokemon switches out (in Gen 3+ Toxic counter resets on switch).
toxic_badly_poisoned.proc_sources: Toxic move (TM06) - 85% acc, dedicated stall move.

ability_immunity: Immunity (Snorlax, Zangoose, Seviper... actually only Snorlax in Gen 3)
Poison-type Pokemon are immune to Poison and Toxic
Steel-type Pokemon are immune to Poison and Toxic

freeze.effect: Pokemon cannot move at all - turns are skipped.
freeze.duration: Until thawed. Each turn ~20% chance to thaw, OR thaws when hit by a Fire-type move.
freeze.proc_sources: Ice Beam 10%, Blizzard 10%, Ice Punch 10%. Fire-type Pokemon are IMMUNE to freeze.
freeze.note: Freeze is rare on the player but devastating. Always carry Aspear Berries against Glacia.

confusion.effect: Pokemon may attack itself instead of the chosen move.
confusion.self_hit_chance_gen3: 33% per turn (1/3 in Gen 3; changed to 50% in Gen 7).
confusion.self_damage_calc: Pokemon attacks itself with a typeless 40-power physical move using its own Atk vs its own Def. No STAB, no critical hits.
confusion.proc_sources: Confuse Ray 100% acc, Supersonic 55% acc, Confusion 10%, Outrage at end of effect, Petal Dance at end of effect, Thrash at end of effect.

healing_items.tip: Best held item for Sidney's gym (Swagger spam)

healing_items.effect: Held; cures any status including confusion

attraction.effect: Pokemon may be immobilized by love instead of attacking.
attraction.gender_requirement: Only works against the OPPOSITE gender. Same-gender = no effect. Genderless Pokemon = no effect.
attraction.duration: Until either Pokemon switches out or faints.
attraction.proc_sources: Attract move (TM45) - 100% acc, Cute Charm ability (30% on contact).

healing_items.effect: Cures attraction (and confusion). Only one in the game (rare)

attraction.tip: Carry a same-gender or Oblivious Pokemon vs Norman's Slaking and Flannery's Torkoal.

lum_berry_universal.effect: Held item; cures any status condition the Pokemon currently has, including confusion and infatuation. One-time use.
lum_berry_universal.where_to_get: Berry trees in Route 123, Route 119, Berry Master gift
lum_berry_universal.tip: Replant Lum Berries on Soft Soil patches; harvest 2-4 per tree. Pre-load every Frontier team with Lum Berries.

best_status_setup_moves.tip: Powerful but inaccurate. Pair with No Guard if available (no Gen 3 Pokemon has it natively in Hoenn).
100% acc Sleep. Top-tier. Breloom is the staple Frontier sleep-setter.
100% acc paralysis - cuts opponent Speed to 25%.

best_status_setup_moves.users: Many - put it on a wall
best_status_setup_moves.tip: Stall pokemon's bread and butter. Milotic with Recover + Toxic = unkillable.

best_status_setup_moves.users: Dusclops, Banette (no, Banette only via Egg Move), Gengar, Spiritomb (not Gen 3)
best_status_setup_moves.tip: 75% acc Burn. Halve enemy Atk. Best counter to physical sweepers.

best_status_setup_moves.users: Gengar, Misdreavus (not Gen 3), Mawile, several Ghost types
best_status_setup_moves.tip: 100% acc confusion. Stack with Attract for maximum chaos.

abilities_in_gen3.overview: Gen 3 (Ruby/Sapphire/Emerald) introduced abilities to the franchise. Each Pokemon has one ability slot (no Hidden Abilities yet - those come in Gen 5). Some abilities are battle-changing.

key_abilities.effect: Immune to Ground-type moves and damage from Spikes/sandstorm-tier hazards (sandstorm doesn't damage immune types).
key_abilities.tip: Flygon is the premier Earthquake user that ALSO doesn't fear EQ from teammates in doubles. Levitate Pokemon do not lose Levitate to Mold Breaker (Mold Breaker is Gen 4).

key_abilities.effect: On switch-in, lowers opposing Pokemon's Atk by 1 stage.
users_in_emerald: Makuhita line - WRONG, Makuhita has Thick Fat / Guts
key_abilities.correction: Common error: Makuhita has Thick Fat / Guts, not Intimidate. Verify list: Mightyena, Salamence, and that's it for native Hoenn.
key_abilities.tip: Lead with Mightyena or Salamence to weaken physical attackers immediately. Stacks with switching - swap out and back to re-trigger.

key_abilities.effect: Opponents using attacks against this Pokemon use 2 PP per move instead of 1. Slowly drains opposing PP.
key_abilities.tip: Useful in long Frontier battles where opponents may run out of damage moves and resort to Struggle.

key_abilities.effect: If this Pokemon is statused, the opponent gets the same status (poison, burn, paralysis - NOT sleep or freeze).
key_abilities.secondary_effect: If your Synchronize Pokemon is the lead in your party, the wild encounters have a 50% chance to share its Nature.
key_abilities.tip: Set a Synchronize Ralts (with desired nature, e.g. Adamant for catching offensive legendaries) as lead before encountering Latios/Regis.

key_abilities.effect: On switch-in, copies the opponent's ability for the duration of the battle.
key_abilities.tip: Trace a Drizzle Kyogre or Drought Groudon to gain weather. Trace a Wonder Guard Shedinja to become impossible to hurt.

key_abilities.effect: Immune to OHKO moves (Sheer Cold, Fissure, Horn Drill, Guillotine). Does NOT prevent normal damage in Gen 3 (Sturdy as 'survive at 1HP' is Gen 5+).
users_in_emerald: Aron line (Aron/Lairon/Aggron also has Rock Head)
key_abilities.tip: Useful vs Articuno-style Sheer Cold spam. In Gen 3, Sturdy does not save you from a one-shot Earthquake.

key_abilities.effect: Only super-effective moves can damage this Pokemon. Everything else does 0 damage. Status moves and indirect damage (poison, burn, weather) DO bypass Wonder Guard.
key_abilities.how_to_get_shedinja: Evolve Nincada at Lv 20 with an empty party slot AND a spare Pokeball. Shedinja appears in the empty slot, with Wonder Guard. Ninjask occupies its old slot.
key_abilities.tip: Shedinja has 1 HP and is fragile. Counters: Toxic, Will-O-Wisp, Sandstorm/Hail damage, any super-effective move. Best in Frontier as a Sturdy-but-perfect wall against opponents that can't hit it super effectively.

key_abilities.effect: +1 Speed stage at the end of each turn while in battle. Compounds quickly.
users_in_emerald: Yanma (not native to Hoenn; trade in from FRLG via Sevii)
key_abilities.tip: Ninjask + Substitute + Baton Pass is a Frontier strategy. Stack 6 Speed boosts then pass to Salamence/Metagross.

key_abilities.effect: On switch-in, summons rain for the rest of the battle (no 5/8-turn timer in Gen 3 - it's permanent until another weather replaces it).
key_abilities.tip: Permanent rain doubles Water move damage and halves Fire. Hits Thunder accuracy to 100%. Frontier rain teams revolve around Kyogre.

key_abilities.effect: On switch-in, summons sun for the rest of the battle (permanent in Gen 3).
key_abilities.tip: Sun doubles Fire damage and halves Water. Boosts SolarBeam (no charge turn). Counter: Rain Dance from a partner.

key_abilities.effect: On switch-in, summons sandstorm for the rest of the battle (permanent in Gen 3).
users_in_emerald: Tyranitar (Larvitar line - not native to Hoenn; trade in from Colosseum/XD)
key_abilities.tip: Sand boosts Rock-type Special Defense by 50%. Damages non-Rock/Ground/Steel types each turn.

key_abilities.effect: While it's raining, recover 1/16 max HP per turn.
users_in_emerald: Ludicolo (Lotad line - Rain Dish OR Swift Swim)
key_abilities.tip: Pair Ludicolo with Kyogre for permanent Rain Dish heal. Niche but cute.

key_abilities.effect: Immune to Fire-type moves. When hit by one, gains a +50% damage boost on its own Fire moves for the rest of the battle.
users_in_emerald: verify_in_game: native Hoenn Flash Fire user
key_abilities.tip: Switch into a predicted Fire move to boost your own Fire offense. Niche but powerful in Fire-heavy meta.

key_abilities.effect: Immune to Electric moves. When hit by one, recover 1/4 max HP.
users_in_emerald: Lanturn (Chinchou line - Surskit/Mareep are NOT Volt Absorb; verify_in_game)
key_abilities.tip: Free heal vs Wattson's gym mid-fight.

key_abilities.effect: Immune to Water moves. When hit by one, recover 1/4 max HP.
When the Pokemon's HP drops below 1/3 max, its same-type STAB moves get a 50% damage boost.
key_abilities.tip: Stay in the red zone for the boost - controlled risk. Sash + low HP would be strong (Sash is Gen 4+ only).

key_abilities.effect: Always able to escape from wild Pokemon, regardless of trapping moves or speed.
key_abilities.tip: Useful for Safari Zone navigation - though Safari Zone has its own escape mechanic.

key_abilities.effect: 10% chance after a battle to find an item, scaled to the Pokemon's level.
users_in_emerald: Linoone (recommended; one Linoone with Pickup carries entire campaign)
key_abilities.tip: Train a Linoone to Lv 100 and sweep Trainer Hill or Frontier rentals. Free Rare Candies, Nuggets, and PP-Ups stack up.

ability_quirks: Truant (Slaking): every other turn loafs (skip move). Trade-off for Slaking's incredible base stats.
Hustle (Togekiss not Gen 3, Pinsir? Verify): boosts Atk 1.5x, drops accuracy 0.8x.
Vital Spirit (Electabuzz, Magmar): immunity to sleep.
Marvel Scale (Milotic): boosts Defense 1.5x while statused. Self-Toxic for permanent boost.
Cute Charm (Skitty, Delcatty): 30% chance to attract opposite-gender attacker on contact.

friendship_happiness.overview: Friendship (a.k.a. Happiness) is a hidden 0-255 value per Pokemon. It controls Return move power, Frustration move power, and friendship-based evolutions. Some species start with high friendship; others with low.
friendship_happiness.scale: 0 to 255. 0 = absolute hatred, 255 = perfect bond. Most Pokemon start at 70.

starting_values_by_source.wild_caught: 70 (most species). Some legendaries start at 0 due to hostility.
starting_values_by_source.egg_hatched: 120 (high - bonus for hatching) but some sources say 70 + walking bonus; verify_in_game
starting_values_by_source.gift_pokemon: Varies. Wynaut egg from Lavaridge starts at hatched value (120-ish).
starting_values_by_source.traded_pokemon: 70 baseline; trades reset friendship in some games but not Gen 3.
specific_low_starters: verify_in_game: any Hoenn-native Pokemon starts particularly low/high

how_to_raise_friendship.action: Walk 256 steps with Pokemon in party
Level up Pokemon (any way - candy or battle)
Use a vitamin (HP Up, Protein, Iron, Calcium, Zinc, Carbos)
Win a battle in Lilycove Contest (any rank)
Get a haircut at... no haircuts in Hoenn

how_to_raise_friendship.gain: All friendship gains are multiplied by 1.5x (rounded down)

how_to_raise_friendship.action: Use a Luxury Ball at capture
how_to_raise_friendship.gain: Doubles ALL friendship gains. Stacks with Soothe Bell.

how_to_raise_friendship.action: Faint a Pokemon - WAIT, fainting LOWERS friendship

how_friendship_drops.action: Use Energy Powder (bitter herbal heal item)
Trade Pokemon - some Gens reset, but Gen 3 keeps friendship intact

soothe_bell.effect: Held item; multiplies friendship gains by 1.5x. Does not stack with itself.
soothe_bell.where_to_get: Mr. Briney's house in Pacifidlog Town (after he retires from sailing duties), or Lilycove Dept Store after gym 8 (verify_in_game).
soothe_bell.tip: Always hold a Soothe Bell on a Pokemon you're evolving by friendship (Crobat, Pikachu, Marill).

luxury_ball.effect: Capture ball that doubles friendship gain for the captured Pokemon.
luxury_ball.where_to_get: Lilycove Dept Store (1000), or Pickup ability rare drop.
luxury_ball.tip: Use Luxury Ball on Zubat (for Crobat). Stack with Soothe Bell - 1.5x * 2x = 3x friendship gain per event. Crobat in 50 walks instead of 150.

evolutions: Azurill -> Marill: needs friendship >= 220 (despite shy flavor)

moves.tip: At max friendship 255, Return is 102 BP STAB-able. Best move for high-friendship physical attackers like Vigoroth/Linoone/Crobat.
Inverse of Return. Use with low-friendship Pokemon. Some traded Pokemon start with reduced friendship - good Frustration target.
